choices.text: Mobile-first: bare `text-base` applies at all sizes (including mobile). `md:text-lg` ADDS the larger size from the md breakpoint (768px) and up. So mobile sees text-base; tablets and up see text-lg. Read responsive classes as scaling UP from the mobile baseline. Default Tailwind breakpoints (sm 640, md 768, lg 1024, xl 1280, 2xl 1536) work min-width style; bare classes are 'always'; prefixed are 'from this breakpoint up.'

questions.explanation: Mobile-first: bare = always; prefix = from that breakpoint up. text-base everywhere; text-lg adds from md.

questions.prompt: Card grid: 1 col mobile, 2 tablet, 3 desktop, 4 large. Clean approach?

choices.text: `text-sm sm:text-base md:text-lg lg:text-xl xl:text-2xl` style chain.

`grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 xl:grid-cols-4 gap-6`. Clean, readable, follows mobile-first. Alternative for truly fluid: `grid grid-cols-[repeat(auto-fit,minmax(280px,1fr))] gap-6` — columns auto-fit based on available space, no explicit breakpoints needed. Auto-fit is great when you don't care about exact column counts and just want a responsive card grid. Pick based on whether you need precise control at each breakpoint or just 'as many columns as fit nicely.'

questions.explanation: grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 xl:grid-cols-4 — or auto-fit for breakpoint-less.
